title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Placed config directory. Sets default thresholds used datasets unless dataset overrides .
code: snapshot_db:       "data/snapshots.sqlite" report_output_dir: "reports/"  default_rules:   # Proportion of non-empty values that must parse as numeric for a column to   # be classified as numeric (affects type inference across many checks)   type_inference_threshold: 0.90    # FAIL if any column's missing-value rate exceeds this (0-1)   max_missing_rate: 0.05    # FAIL if non-numeric values in a numeric column exceed this rate   max_non_numeric_rate: 0.01    # FAIL if row count drops below this (0 = disabled)   min_row_count: 0   # max_row_count: 1000000       # optional FAIL if row count exceeds this   # max_file_size_mb: 500        # optional FAIL if file size exceeds this    # Outlier detection (QC-15): both thresholds are optional; omit to skip   # max_z_score: 3.0   # iqr_fence_multiplier: 1.5    # Version-comparison thresholds (used when a previous file is available)   max_row_count_change_pct:       0.10   max_numeric_mean_shift_pct:     0.20   max_missing_rate_change_pp:     2.0    # percentage points   max_non_numeric_rate_change_pp: 1.0   missing_rate_change_severity:   warn   # set to "fail" to escalate CP-03 breaches to FAIL    flag_new_columns:         true   # set false to suppress new-column warnings in CP-02   flag_dropped_columns:     true   # set false to suppress dropped-column warnings in CP-02   flag_type_changes:        true   # set false to suppress type-change warnings in CP-02   flag_column_order_change: true   # set false to skip CP-08 entirely   column_order_severity:    warn   # set to "fail" to escalate CP-08 breaches to FAIL for CSV

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: One file per dataset. keys specify needed — everything else inherits global defaults.
code: dataset_name: "customer_accounts"   # for human reference only — the functional                                      # identifier is the YAML filename, which must                                      # match the dataset_name argument to run_dq_check()  # --- File location --- # Option A: folder scan (two most recently modified files are used) folder: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/"  # Option B: explicit paths (set current_file only → single-file mode, #           no version comparison) # current_file:  "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260410.csv" # previous_file: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260401.csv"  format:    csv encoding:  "UTF-8" delimiter: ","  # --- Schema contract (optional) --- # SC-01 flags extra columns; SC-02 flags missing columns. expected_columns:   - id   - name   - country_code   - account_status   - account_balance   - created_date  # --- Key columns (optional) --- # QC-12 checks each for duplicate values. key_columns:   - id  # --- Rule overrides (optional) --- # These replace the matching keys in default_rules for this dataset only. rule_overrides:   max_missing_rate:        0.02   max_row_count_change_pct: 0.05   min_row_count:           1000  # --- Per-column rules (optional) --- column_rules:   country_code:     allowed_values: ["GB", "US", "DE", "FR"]   # QC-09     pattern: "^[A-Z]{2}$"                       # QC-13   account_status:     allowed_values: ["ACTIVE", "CLOSED", "SUSPENDED"]   account_balance:     min_value: 0                                 # QC-10     max_value: 1000000  # --- Custom checks (optional) --- # custom_checks_file: "custom/customer_accounts_checks.R"  # --- Output paths (optional, override global) --- # snapshot_db:       "data/snapshots.sqlite" # report_output_dir: "reports/"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: infer_col_type() classifies column whitespace trimming. Rules applied priority order: date — non-empty values parse known date format (%Y-%m-%d, %d/%m/%Y, %m/%d/%Y, %Y%m%d, %d-%m-%Y) numeric — ≥ type_inference_threshold non-empty values coerce numeric character — everything else unknown — values empty NA threshold defaults 90%, meaning column 10% bad values still classified numeric, making bad values visible QC-11 (non-numeric contamination). configurable per dataset via type_inference_threshold default_rules rule_overrides: Changing threshold affects QC-06, QC-07, QC-08, QC-11, CP-02, CP-04, CP-05, CP-06, CP-07. Note changing runs may cause CP-02 report spurious type change columns sit close old new thresholds, even underlying data change.
code: # raise threshold — stricter, fewer columns classified as numeric rule_overrides:   type_inference_threshold: 0.95  # lower threshold — more lenient, columns with more noise still classified numeric rule_overrides:   type_inference_threshold: 0.80 infer_col_type(c("2024-01-15", "2024-06-30"))  # "date" infer_col_type(c("100", "200", "N/A"))          # "character" (only 67% numeric) infer_col_type(c("100", "200", "N/A", rep("50", 17)))  # "numeric" (95% numeric) infer_col_type(c(NA, "", NA))                   # "unknown" # with a custom threshold: infer_col_type(c(rep("1", 17), "a", "b", "c"), threshold = 0.80)  # "numeric" (85%)

code: result <- run_dq_check(   dataset_name = "customer_accounts",   config_dir   = "path/to/configs",   # contains dqcheckr.yml + customer_accounts.yml   open_report  = TRUE                 # open HTML in browser when interactive )  # Console output (always printed): # [dqcheckr] customer_accounts: FAIL - 1 warning(s), 2 failure(s). Report: reports/...html  result$status       # "PASS", "WARN", or "FAIL" result$report_path  # full path to the HTML file result$snapshot_id  # integer row ID in snapshots table

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Every check function exported can called directly, without going full pipeline. checks share signature — (df, config) — return list dq_result objects.
code: library(dqcheckr)  # Build a minimal config programmatically cfg <- list(   rules = list(     max_missing_rate     = 0.05,     max_non_numeric_rate = 0.01,     min_row_count        = 0   ),   column_rules     = list(     status = list(allowed_values = c("ACTIVE", "CLOSED"))   ),   key_columns      = "id",   expected_columns = NULL )  df <- read.csv("data/customer_accounts.csv", colClasses = "character") df[] <- lapply(df, trimws)  # Run any individual check check_missing_rate(df, cfg)       # QC-01 check_empty_column(df, cfg)       # QC-02 check_duplicate_rows(df, cfg)     # QC-03 check_row_count(df, cfg)          # QC-04  (INFO) check_col_count(df, cfg)          # QC-05  (INFO) check_inferred_types(df, cfg)     # QC-06  (INFO) check_numeric_stats(df, cfg)      # QC-07  (INFO) check_distinct_counts(df, cfg)    # QC-08  (INFO) check_allowed_values(df, cfg)     # QC-09 check_numeric_bounds(df, cfg)     # QC-10 check_non_numeric(df, cfg)        # QC-11 check_key_uniqueness(df, cfg)     # QC-12 check_pattern(df, cfg)            # QC-13 check_min_row_count(df, cfg)      # QC-14 check_outliers(df, cfg)           # QC-15 check_file_encoding(df, cfg)      # QC-16 check_schema_contract(df, cfg)    # SC-01 / SC-02  # Inspect a result r <- check_missing_rate(df, cfg)[[1]] r$status    # "PASS" or "FAIL" r$observed  # e.g. "3.2% missing (4 of 125)"  # Run all single-snapshot checks at once all_results <- run_qc_checks(df, cfg)  # Overall status across any result list overall_status(all_results)  # "FAIL" > "WARN" > "PASS" > "INFO"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Create plain .R file defines custom_checks(df). receives trimmed data frame must return list dq_result objects. Point dataset config: file sourced isolated environment (global session) whose parent baseenv(). dq_result() explicitly available without qualification. dqcheckr functions (e.g. resolve_col_type(), infer_col_type()) must called package prefix: dqcheckr::resolve_col_type(). Standard base R functions fully available. error — missing file, undefined function, runtime failure — stops run clear message. Tip: always set column = check scoped specific column. Results column = NA (default) appear HTML report contribute overall status, written column_snapshots SQLite table therefore appear per-column trend queries.
code: # File: custom/customer_accounts_checks.R  custom_checks <- function(df) {   results <- list()    # Rule: ACTIVE accounts must not have a zero balance   active_zero <- df[df$account_status == "ACTIVE" &                     !is.na(df$account_balance) &                     df$account_balance == "0", ]   n <- nrow(active_zero)   results <- c(results, list(dq_result(     check_id   = "CUST-01",     check_name = "No zero-balance active accounts",     column     = "account_balance",   # enables per-column storage in SQLite     status     = if (n > 0) "FAIL" else "PASS",     observed   = sprintf("%d ACTIVE account(s) with balance 0", n),     message    = if (n > 0)       sprintf("%d ACTIVE account(s) have a zero balance.", n)     else       "No ACTIVE accounts have a zero balance."   )))    results } custom_checks_file: "custom/customer_accounts_checks.R"

title: Getting started with dqcheckr
text: Every run writes two rows shared SQLite database: one row snapshots (run-level summary) one row per column-stat column_snapshots. datasets share one database, can query across : output/ directory (database file) created automatically first run exist.
code: library(DBI) library(RSQLite)  con <- dbConnect(SQLite(), "data/snapshots.sqlite")  # Recent runs for one dataset dbGetQuery(con,   "SELECT id, file_name, overall_status, check_fail_count, run_timestamp    FROM snapshots    WHERE dataset_name = 'customer_accounts'    ORDER BY id DESC    LIMIT 10")  # Column-level stats for the most recent run dbGetQuery(con,   "SELECT column_name, dq_check, value, threshold    FROM column_snapshots    WHERE snapshot_id = (      SELECT MAX(id) FROM snapshots WHERE dataset_name = 'customer_accounts'    )    ORDER BY column_name, dq_check")  dbDisconnect(con) dbGetQuery(con,   "SELECT dataset_name, COUNT(*) AS runs,           SUM(check_fail_count) AS total_failures    FROM snapshots    GROUP BY dataset_name")

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Row count: large change (increase decrease) flagged. Schema: new columns, dropped columns, type changes flagged. Missing rates: columns proportion missing values increased significantly flagged. Numeric distributions: columns average value shifted significantly flagged. Non-numeric contamination: columns proportion bad (non-numeric) values increased flagged. Distinct values: new values appeared values disappeared categorical columns listed. Column order: change order columns warning CSV files error fixed-width files (column order determines parsing).

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: HTML report — one file per run, self-contained (internet required view). Contains: header showing dataset name, file name, run time, overall status badge (green = clear, amber = warnings, red = failures). file summary (row count, column count, file size, format, encoding). quality checks table listing every check, status, observed, threshold, plain-English explanation. Failures warnings appear first. custom checks table (configured). version comparison section showing changed since previous delivery, plain-English narrative summary top. historical trend section showing row count check outcomes across last 10 runs (appears least two runs recorded). appendix full column-level statistics table. SQLite database — single database shared across datasets. Records summary statistics every run, enabling long-term trend queries without reading source files .

code: snapshot_db: "data/snapshots.sqlite" report_output_dir: "reports/"  default_rules:   # Single-snapshot thresholds   max_missing_rate: 0.05            # FAIL if column missing rate exceeds this   max_non_numeric_rate: 0.01        # FAIL if non-numeric rate in numeric column exceeds this   min_row_count: 0                  # FAIL if row count below this (0 = disabled)   # max_row_count: 1000000          # optional FAIL if row count exceeds this   # max_file_size_mb: 500           # optional FAIL if file size exceeds this   # max_z_score: 3.0                # optional QC-15 outlier detection (Z-score)   # iqr_fence_multiplier: 1.5       # optional QC-15 outlier detection (IQR fence)    # Version comparison thresholds   max_row_count_change_pct: 0.10   max_numeric_mean_shift_pct: 0.20   max_missing_rate_change_pp: 2.0   # percentage points   max_non_numeric_rate_change_pp: 1.0   missing_rate_change_severity: warn  # "warn" or "fail" — severity for CP-03 breaches    # Schema flags   flag_new_columns: true            # set false to suppress new-column warnings in CP-02   flag_dropped_columns: true        # set false to suppress dropped-column warnings in CP-02   flag_type_changes: true           # set false to suppress type-change warnings in CP-02   flag_column_order_change: true    # set false to skip CP-08 entirely   column_order_severity: warn       # "warn" or "fail" — overrides format default for CP-08 dataset_name: "customer_accounts" folder: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/" format: csv                 # csv | fwf encoding: "UTF-8"          # ASCII (a lossless UTF-8 subset) is read as UTF-8;                             #   UTF-8 files are validity-scanned per delivery (QC-16) delimiter: ","              # csv only quote_char: '"'             # csv only — quote character (default ") col_names: ~                # csv only — explicit column names (overrides the                             #   file header); required for headerless files csv_skip: 0                 # csv only — leading lines to drop (default 0). Set                             #   to 1 with col_names to replace an unusable header                             #   row, e.g. one that repeats column names. fwf_widths: ~               # fwf only fwf_col_names: ~            # fwf only fwf_skip: 0                 # fwf only — header rows to skip (default 0)  # Optional: explicit file paths override folder-scan version detection # current_file: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260410.csv" # previous_file: "data/incoming/customer_accounts/20260401.csv"  # Optional: schema contract expected_columns:   - id   - name   - country_code   - account_status   - account_balance   - created_date  # Optional: key columns that must be unique key_columns:   - id  # Optional: pin a column's logical type instead of inferring it (see C.5). # Each value must be one of character, numeric, date (any other aborts with # dqcheckr_invalid_config). column_types:   account_id: character   opened_on: date  # Optional rule overrides (merged over global defaults) rule_overrides:   max_missing_rate: 0.02   max_row_count_change_pct: 0.05   max_non_numeric_rate: 0.005   min_row_count: 1000  # Optional per-column rules column_rules:   country_code:     allowed_values: ["GB", "US", "DE", "FR"]     pattern: "^[A-Z]{2}$"   account_status:     allowed_values: ["ACTIVE", "CLOSED", "SUSPENDED"]   account_balance:     min_value: 0     max_value: 1000000  # Optional custom checks custom_checks_file: "custom/customer_accounts_checks.R"

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification
text: Two modes: Folder scan (default): two recently modified files folder used. Newest = current, second-newest = previous. Explicit naming: current_file (optionally previous_file) set dataset config, paths used directly. Useful file naming conventions reflect delivery order, re-running check specific pair files. current file available (either via folder scan explicit config), run proceeds single-file mode: version comparison vs-previous schema checks skipped.

title: dqcheckr — Software Specification

Rules applied order: Known date formats tested: %Y-%m-%d, %d/%m/%Y, %m/%d/%Y, %Y%m%d, %d-%m-%Y. type_inference_threshold defaults 0.90 configurable default_rules (per-dataset rule_overrides). default, column 10% bad (non-numeric) values still classified numeric, making bad values visible QC-11. column’s type may also pinned explicitly column_types (see C.2), bypasses inference entirely.

what: dqcheckr — Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
title: dqcheckr — Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
text: Automated data quality checks recurring dataset deliveries. new file arrival, dqcheckr runs battery quality checks, compares file previous delivery, writes self-contained HTML report, records summary statistics local SQLite database quality trends can tracked time. Supports CSV fixed-width formats. Custom organisation-specific checks can supplied plain R files. CLI/API package — UI. ’d rather configure run checks without writing R code, see dqcheckrGUI, Shiny front-end built top package.

title: dqcheckr — Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
text: Runs single-snapshot quality checks (schema, missing rates, types, patterns, bounds, ) incoming file Compares file previous delivery (row/column differences, drift) Writes self-contained HTML report run Records summary statistics SQLite snapshot database long-term trend tracking historical drift comparison Lets supply organisation-specific checks plain R files

title: dqcheckr — Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
code: install.packages("dqcheckr")  # or, the development version from GitHub devtools::install_github("mickmioduszewski/dqcheckr")

title: dqcheckr — Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries
text: data officer runs single command arriving dataset: prints one-line console summary, writes HTML report, returns list(status, report_path, snapshot_id) invisibly. Two YAML files control every run: global dqcheckr.yml (default thresholds shared across datasets) per-dataset <dataset_name>.yml (file location, expected columns, column-level rules overrides).
code: library(dqcheckr)  run_dq_check("customer_accounts", config_dir = "path/to/configs")

title: QC-16: File encoding sanity — check_file_encoding
text: Verifies delivered file's bytes matched encoding declared config. read_dataset scans whole file UTF-8 validity parsing (effective encoding UTF-8) records outcome returned data frame; check turns outcome result: PASS file valid UTF-8 declared,     declared single-byte encoding (e.g. ISO-8859-1,     Windows-1252) made validity scan meaningless – every byte     sequence valid encodings construction. FAIL file valid UTF-8 declared.     run still completes: file read using single-byte fallback     encoding, message reports detector's best guess     actual encoding config can corrected. WARN declared encoding multi-byte unknown     (e.g. UTF-16LE, Shift-JIS): dqcheckr scans UTF-8,     file read declared validity verified –     never reported "valid construction". WARN UTF-8 scan complete (    example memory large delivery): validity unknown,     neither clean PASS definitive FAIL. supplier can change export encoding deliveries, runs every delivery rather configuration time. Returns empty list df come read_dataset (scan outcome report).

what: dqcheckr: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries — dqcheckr-package
title: dqcheckr: Automated Data Quality Checks for Recurring Dataset Deliveries — dqcheckr-package
text: Automates quality verification recurring external dataset deliveries. new file arrival, runs single-snapshot quality checks (QC-01 QC-16, SC-01/SC-02), compares file previous delivery (CP-01 CP-08), writes self-contained 'HTML' report, records summary statistics local 'SQLite' database long-term trend tracking. Supports 'CSV' fixed-width formats. Custom organisation-specific checks can supplied plain R files.

Configuration driven two 'YAML' files: global dqcheckr.yml per-dataset <dataset_name>.yml. packages called report templates rendered Quarto separate process (inst/templates/*.qmd), static analysis R/ see used – without reference , R CMD check reports "Namespaces Imports field imported ".

text: Date formats tried fixed precedence order: "%Y-%m-%d", "%d/%m/%Y", "%m/%d/%Y", "%Y%m%d", "%d-%m-%Y". column classified "date" every non-empty value matches format's exact character shape parses valid calendar date; single malformed date therefore flips whole column "numeric" "character" (flips deliveries surfaced check CP-02c). shape anchored, value trailing characters ("2024-01-15x") extra digits (9-digit "202401159") treated date. Two caveats follow precedence rules: ambiguous day/month values resolve day-first ("%d/%m/%Y" tried "%m/%d/%Y"), -8-digit identifier columns whose values happen valid "%Y%m%d" dates classify dates. Pin type entry column_types config map heuristic gets column wrong.

title: dqcheckr 0.2.5
text: Report filenames now include snapshot id (dataset_20260718_010203_47.html), two runs one dataset start wall-clock second longer collide single filename. Previously second run silently overwrote first run’s report first run’s snapshot kept pointing . filename now written snapshot’s report_file column update report confirmed rendered, rather optimistically insert time, column can never name report written. Configuration lookups longer partial-match. R’s $ operator falls back prefix match exact element absent, config column_rules: key parked mistyped one (column_rules_disabled, column_rules_old, …) section silently drive per-column thresholds – producing wrong PASS/FAIL verdicts section config contain. Every config-key access now uses exact [[ ]] indexing, parked renamed section correctly treated absent. QC-16 longer reports spurious clean pass delivery whose encoding actually verify. declared multi-byte unknown encoding (UTF-16LE, UTF-32, Shift-JIS, GB18030, …) used reported “single-byte encoding; every byte valid construction” – false, also silently disabled crash guard scanning gives UTF-8 deliveries. encoding now read declared reported WARN stating validity-checked. Genuine single-byte encodings (ISO-8859-x, Windows-125x) still PASS. UTF-8 validity scan now streams file bounded chunks instead reading one -memory vector, arbitrarily large delivery verified flat memory rather exhausting network-share hosts dqcheckr deploys . Non-finite values numeric column (Inf/-Inf, e.g. corrupted upstream delivery whose CSV contains literal text “Inf”) longer mishandled. compute_col_stats() excludes mean, standard deviation, minimum, maximum, snapshot database can longer store literal string “NaN”/“Inf” poison later drift comparisons; finite mean shift contaminated delivery causes still reported drift. check_outliers() (QC-15), previously aborted uninformative “missing value TRUE/FALSE needed” column, now runs cleanly. zero-column delivery (example empty file) now recorded. Previously compute_col_stats() returned NULL, snapshot write failed lost entire run; run now snapshotted column count zero. Failures previously swallowed silent, benign-looking results now surfaced. read_recent_snapshots() list_snapshots() longer report corrupt, locked, unreadable database empty history. emit warning naming cause returning empty frame, “read failed” distinguishable “runs yet”. snapshot write fails, run_dq_check() now appends “[snapshot recorded]” result line instead printing unqualified success history row lost. QC-16 longer reports confident PASS UTF-8 validity scan fails (example, memory large delivery). reports WARN stating encoding verified, rather PASS whose rationale wrongly called UTF-8 “single-byte encoding, valid construction”. Concurrent runs sharing one snapshot database longer lose snapshot die migration. Connections now set PRAGMA busy_timeout, run meets database another run writing waits lock instead failing instantly (snapshot previously swallowed warning). Schema creation auto-migration older databases now run inside single BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction, two first-runs upgrade can longer add column (loser used die “duplicate column name”). WAL journalling intentionally used, unsafe network file systems dqcheckr deployed . init_snapshot_db() longer modifies snapshots table create. target database already contains unrelated table name, now aborts typed dqcheckr_schema_error instead altering user’s table. Column-existence checks migration now case-insensitive, matching SQLite, column stored e.g. Report_File recognised rather re-added. drift report now flags missing-rate non-numeric-rate change direction corresponding comparison check. .compute_drift() columns used abs(), column improved deliveries (fewer missing values, less non-numeric junk) flagged breaching, CP-03 CP-07 checks – reading max_missing_rate_change_pp / max_non_numeric_rate_change_pp thresholds – passed . Drift now breaches increase, matching checks. (numeric-mean-shift drift keeps two-directional test, matching two-directional CP-04 check.) run whose HTML report produced longer records successful-looking snapshot. Quarto CLI absent report skipped warning, snapshot row previously kept render_status = "success" report_file naming report never written, history readers (GUI’s “Open report” link) pointed file 404s. snapshot now reconciled actually happened: report file exists row marked render_status = "failed" report_file cleared. Rendering returns without producing file now raises instead reporting success, report_file guaranteed name report exists. Column type inference longer misclassifies value whose prefix happens date. .Date() matches prefix silently ignores trailing characters, "2024-01-15x" 9-digit id "202401159" (whose first eight digits parse %Y%m%d) classified "date" — made corrupt dates pass QC-06 stripped numeric checks (QC-07/08/11) id columns eight digits. date format now gated anchored shape calendar validation. documented caveat unchanged: genuine eight-digit value also valid %Y%m%d date still classifies date; nine-digit id now correctly classifies numeric. read_recent_snapshots() now returns full set columns even snapshot database created 0.2.3. Previously ran SELECT * without migrating, older database returned rows newer columns (report_file) absent rather NA, callers relying columns errored instead degrading. missing columns now filled read defaults migration apply; database file modified, reads remain safe read-network-shared databases.

Missing rates defined 0 empty inputs, QC-14 gains unconditional “Empty file” FAIL sub-check empty delivery always fails run — snapshot report — instead crashing . Snapshot writes now wrapped single transaction, failure can longer leave snapshots row without column_snapshots stats; column-level custom results batch-inserted. snapshot run_timestamp report filename now derived one timestamp taken start run. Previously came two separate clock reads, links reconstruct report filename snapshot timestamp (GUI ) intermittently point file name one second . compare_snapshots() longer announces (offers open) drift report path Quarto unavailable report written. Explicitly supplied snapshot IDs now validated belong requested dataset; previously IDs another dataset silently produced cross-dataset “drift” comparison. Rendered reports moved render directory copy+delete fallback file.rename() fails across filesystems (e.g. network-share report directory); previously move failed silently. QC-10 (numeric bounds) now counts violating rows rather distinct values — million rows --range value longer reads “1 --range value(s)”. QC-09 (allowed values) compares numerically YAML rule supplies numbers, file value "2.10" matches allowed value 2.1. read_recent_snapshots()’s empty-database fallback now columns live query (missing comparison_mode, render_status, type_changed_cols_vs_previous).

title: dqcheckr 0.2.0
text: New compare_snapshots() list_snapshots() functions drift analysis. Compares two historical snapshots optionally renders HTML drift report per-column statistical drift, schema changes, trend charts. New resolve_col_type() function: returns effective type column, respecting per-column type overrides set column_types config key. New QC-15 outlier detection check (check_outliers()). Configured via max_z_score /iqr_fence_multiplier; skipped silently neither set. check_key_uniqueness() (QC-12) now supports composite keys: set key_columns character vector dataset YAML. check_min_row_count() (QC-14) gains max_row_count max_file_size_mb thresholds. col_threshold() table_threshold() added internal helpers; column_rules per-column threshold overrides now correctly stored column_snapshots.threshold (G-01).

title: dqcheckr 0.2.0
text: Reports migrated rmarkdown Quarto. render_report() uses quarto::quarto_render() returns NULL warning Quarto CLI installed. compare_schema() (CP-02) split three separate result objects: CP-02a (new columns), CP-02b (dropped columns), CP-02c (type changes). compare_non_numeric_rate() (CP-07) now always emits result every eligible column, including PASS columns rate increase (G-02). run_timestamp now stored ISO-8601 UTC format (2026-01-01T12:00:00Z) rather local time (RC-07). snapshots table gains three columns first use: comparison_mode, render_status, type_changed_cols_vs_previous. Existing 0.1.x databases auto-migrated first 0.2.0 run. SQLite foreign key enforcement now explicitly enabled every connection (PRAGMA foreign_keys = ). detect_files() uses filename secondary sort two files share modification time, making folder-mode ordering deterministic (RC-01). check_allowed_values() (QC-09), compare_new_values() (CP-05), compare_dropped_values() (CP-06) cap observed 20 values "... N " suffix (RC-04, RC-05). check_non_numeric() (QC-11) gains warn_non_numeric_rate config key separate WARN threshold (C-01). compare_missing_rate() (CP-03) gains missing_rate_change_severity config key (warn / fail) (B-07). compare_column_order() (CP-08) gains column_order_severity config key overrides format-based default (C-02). compute_col_stats() stores numeric mean key numeric_parseable_mean (renamed numeric_mean) clarify non-parseable values excluded calculation (C-04). Comparison summary HTML report now lists FAIL/WARN messages bullet list rather picking single worst result (C-05). compare_snapshots() uses full merged per-dataset config drift threshold comparisons, *** markers match original check run datasets rule_overrides (G-05/G-06). compute_col_stats() unused qc_results parameter removed (D-01).

title: dqcheckr 0.1.1
text: flag_new_columns, flag_dropped_columns, flag_type_changes (CP-02) flag_column_order_change (CP-08) now honoured. Setting flag false suppresses corresponding check report. Schema changes still tracked SQLite snapshot regardless flags. type_inference_threshold now configurable per dataset via rule_overrides dataset YAML (default_rules global config). Previously fixed 90%, now defaults 90% set. Affects QC-06, QC-07, QC-08, QC-11, CP-02, CP-04, CP-05, CP-06, CP-07.

title: dqcheckr 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Single-snapshot quality checks: QC-01 QC-14 (missing rate, empty columns, duplicate rows, row/column counts, inferred types, numeric stats, distinct counts, allowed values, numeric bounds, non-numeric rate, key uniqueness, regex pattern, minimum row count) SC-01/SC-02 (schema contract). Version comparison checks: CP-01 CP-08 (row count change, schema diff, missing rate change, numeric mean shift, new/dropped distinct values, non-numeric rate change, column order). Custom organisation-specific checks via plain R file. Self-contained HTML report check tables, historical trend charts, column statistics appendix. SQLite snapshot database long-term trend tracking. Supports CSV fixed-width (FWF) file formats. Configuration via global dqcheckr.yml per-dataset YAML files.
